title: Fix the Broken Dict Return
description: A refactor changed a dict return to a namedtuple, breaking .get() calls in tests.
pr_diff:
PR Diff:
  - def get_user_profile(user_id):
  -     return {"name": user.name, "age": user.age}
  + from collections import namedtuple
  + Profile = namedtuple("Profile", ["name", "age"])
  + def get_user_profile(user_id):
  +     return Profile(name=user.name, age=user.age)
initial_ci_output:
FAILED tests/test_profile.py::test_profile_name
AttributeError: 'Profile' object has no attribute 'get'

tests/test_profile.py:12: in test_profile_name
    assert get_user_profile(1).get("name") == "Alice"

target_test.failure_output:
FAILED tests/test_profile.py::test_profile_name
AttributeError: 'Profile' object has no attribute 'get'

regression_guard.failure_output: FAILED regression: profile object must support attribute access (profile.name)
regression_guard.success_output: PASSED regression guard: attribute access works
